summary: End-to-end mobile UI testing (375x844 viewport) of the Deeper Connect / AtomOS Expo app. All requested flows pass: Welcome → Scan (radar, 'Deeper non trouvé', manual IP + demo fallback) → Login (>=4 chars enables submit, remember, submit) → PIN setup (mismatch rejected, matching 1234/1234 unlocks) → Dashboard (status header, bandwidth card with sparklines, CPU/RAM/Temp/Devices stat cards, quick toggles, demo banner, bottom tabs) → DPN (smart/full segmented control, country picker modal with country-pick-FR/US/JP/DE, add-tunnel flow, tunnel-remove buttons, reboot confirm/cancel dialog) → Devices (6 simulated devices each with device-dpn-* and device-block-*) → Settings (theme dark/light, lang FR/EN immediate UI switch, PIN row, biometric switch, IP edit modal with input/save, logout returns to welcome). 30+ testIDs verified.

issues: Mixed Content warnings in console when pingDeeper tries http://34.34.34.34, http://192.168.0.1, http://192.168.1.1 from an HTTPS page. Expected (browsers block, falls into demo). Not a functional bug; consider suppressing/wrapping in try/catch with HTTPS detection to silence console noise on web preview.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: `props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents`. Cosmetic only, originates from a library.
`useNativeDriver` not supported on web — falls back to JS-based animation. Expected on web preview.

action_items: (Optional) Silence Mixed Content errors during scan by short-circuiting pingDeeper on web (typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.location.protocol === 'https:') and going straight to not-found/demo path.
(Optional) Replace deprecated `pointerEvents` prop with `style.pointerEvents` if it originates from your own code (likely from a 3rd-party dep).
critical_code_review_comments: AppContext-driven gating in app/onboarding/login.tsx looks inverted at first glance (`if (pin || biometricEnabled) -> dashboard else -> /lock?setup=1`). Logic is actually correct for first-login flow because pin & biometric are unset on fresh install, sending user to PIN setup. Behaviour confirmed by E2E test (lands on lock setup screen).
Frontend correctly uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL via env (no hardcoded URLs in source).
All interactive elements expose testID props as specified in the request — coverage is excellent.
seed_data_creation: None — DeeperSimulator (src/api/deeper.ts) provides in-memory mock data (6 devices, 5 initial tunnels, bandwidth/HW stats).
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is a frontend-only Expo/React Native app served via web preview at https://secure-gateway-43.preview.emergentagent.com (taken from frontend/.env EXPO_PACKAGER_PROXY_URL). Backend is purely mocked via DeeperSimulator. To test, navigate to /onboarding/welcome to start clean. Login accepts any password >=4 chars (use admin123). PIN: 1234/1234. All testIDs match the contract listed in the review request. Console will emit benign Mixed Content errors from pingDeeper on the scan screen — these are expected on the HTTPS cloud preview and trigger the demo fallback by design.
rca_of_issue: No functional bugs found. Console Mixed Content errors are an expected side-effect of trying http://<lan-ip>/ from an HTTPS page; the app gracefully falls back to demo mode and the user-facing 'Deeper non trouvé' state is reached correctly.
